attributes.description: Alerts when `vcenter.vm.memory.ballooned > 0`. The hypervisor is reclaiming memory from the VM.
attributes.name: [vSphere OTel] VM memory ballooning

investigation_guide.blob:
## vSphere VM memory ballooning

### What fired
`vcenter.vm.memory.ballooned > 0` during the window.

### Why it matters
Ballooning is ESXi pressuring the guest to release memory. Performance within the VM degrades proportional to ballooned size.

### Triage
1. Check host memory utilisation.
2. Inspect VM memory configuration.

### Remediation
- Migrate VM or grow host memory.

### Tuning
- Fires on any ballooned memory.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-vcenterreceiver.otel-*
// VM memory ballooning: host reclaiming memory from VMs indicates memory pressure
// Any non-zero value is a concern; adjust threshold if needed for noise reduction
| WHERE vcenter.vm.memory.ballooned IS NOT NULL AND vcenter.vm.memory.ballooned > 0
| STATS ballooned_mib = MAX(vcenter.vm.memory.ballooned) BY vcenter.datacenter.name, vcenter.cluster.name, vcenter.host.name, vcenter.vm.name
| SORT ballooned_mib DESC
| LIMIT 20
